nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Kyrkeby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Danish and Norwegian: habitational name from a place called Kirkeby (or, in the case of the Norwegian name, from any of ten farmsteads so named), from Old Norse kirkja ‘church’ + býr ‘settlement, farm’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard de Kyrkeby I was born in 1322, in Kirkby, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son. He died about 1378, in his hometown, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard de Kyrkeby I est né en 1322, à Kirkby, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé environ 1378, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Richard de Kyrkeby I wurde 1322 in Kirkby, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb um 1378 im Alter von 57 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Richard de Kyrkeby I è nato il nel 1322 nel Kirkby, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1378, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.Kirkby, Knowsley, Lancashire, England, U: イギリスイギリスランカシュアイアキルクビー
ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Kyrkeby Iは1322年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアキルクビーで生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は約1378年に故郷で57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Kyrkeby I(은)는 1322년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 커k비에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1378년 그의 고향에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard de Kyrkeby I nasceu em 1322, em Kirkby, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1378, em sua cidade natal, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard de Kyrkeby I родился в 1322 г. в местности киркби, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер около 1378 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Richard de Kyrkeby I nació en 1322, en Kirkby, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió aproximadamente 1378, en su pueblo, a la edad de 57 años.
